topTargets.name: Demond Claiborne
topTargets.reasoning: RSP RB3 (DoT 84.5) with LeSean McCoy comp and a 'second-round pick regardless of NFL Draft' tag — perfect 2.04 value for a roster carrying only 4 RBs behind 10 WRs. Likely available after Tate/Love/Lemon/Mendoza tier clears in round 1.

topTargets.name: Emmett Johnson
topTargets.reasoning: RSP RB2 at DoT 84.6 with Harold Fannin trajectory comp; addresses the same RB-thin roster shape and slots cleanly into the $4.3M cap as an active-roster contributor on a rookie deal.

topTargets.name: Jordyn Tyson
topTargets.reasoning: Industry darling (MFL ADP 12.71) at RSP DoT 84.5 — a medium-RSP-affinity GM blends boards, and Tyson's consensus first-round value at 2.04 is hard to pass even with WR depth. Boom-bust profile fits a 'best WR upside left' bet.

topTargets.name: Nicholas Singleton
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 84.1, Penn State pedigree, consensus MFL ADP 25.83 — natural 2.11 target if Claiborne/Johnson are gone in round 2. Doubles down on the RB need without reaching.

topTargets.name: Omar Cooper Jr.
topTargets.reasoning: DoT 83.9, ADP 17.20 — Deebo Samuel comp gives versatility to a deep WR room as a flex/utility piece. Strong 2.11 value blending RSP and consensus.

capPosture: Workable at $4.3M space — can absorb one active rookie deal and stash a second on taxi (50% hit) without restructuring; 3 expirings give breathing room next year.

wildcard.name: Eli Stowers
wildcard.reasoning: RSP TE1 at DoT 84.4 (Vanderbilt move TE) — TE room already has 4 bodies but RSP-leaning GMs chase Stowers' ceiling, and his ADP 21.25 lines up cleanly with the 2.11 slot.

summary: Bring The Pain enters the draft WR-saturated (10 on roster) and RB-light (4), so the 2.04 pick should target the best RB available in the RSP/consensus overlap — Claiborne or Johnson. With $4.3M cap space and a medium RSP affinity, this GM blends boards rather than reaching, and the 2.11 pick becomes a depth swing at either a second RB (Singleton) or a high-upside WR/TE on taxi to defer cap (Cooper Jr., Brazzell, Stowers).
